prompt:
You are extracting atomic facts from a topic document.

The topic document below contains all source text for one specific topic, assembled with provenance headings. Your output must pass all Five Tests.

**Test 1 — Stand-Alone**: A reader with no other facts must fully understand the policy. No implied "who does this apply to?", "when does it apply?", "except what?". Include the subject explicitly in every fact.

**Test 2 — One Topic**: A single noun phrase names the subject. If "and" is needed to name the subject, split into two facts.

**Test 3 — Reconstruction**: Concatenating all your facts must be informationally equivalent to the topic document: same obligations, permissions, exceptions, numerical values. No information omitted, none added.

**Test 4 — Canonical Form**: Active voice. No document-navigation language ("as mentioned in", "refer to", "see above", "this section"). No redundant preamble ("it is important to note that", "this policy states", "please be aware").

**Test 5 — Template-Slot Ready**: No audience-specific framing baked in ("as a parent, you should…", "staff are reminded that…"). No channel-specific framing ("as you read this handbook…").

Instructions:
- This is the only pass that writes content — rewrite source text into canonical form
- Eliminate redundancy across provenance spans (same obligation stated in multiple spans → one fact)
- Split only at true topic boundaries (when the subject noun phrase changes)
- `title`: short noun phrase naming the subject of this fact
- `kind`: assign ONLY for clearly non-policy content — 'rationale', 'principle', or 'context'. Leave null for all policy facts (rules, procedures, fees, contact details, definitions)
- `topic_tags`: kebab-case tags, consistent with the topic_slug used in grouping

Audience scope: parents, staff, students
Channel scope: policy-documents, staff-handbook, parent-handbook

Topic document:
### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

**Not acceptable:**

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts shorter than mid-thigh.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear (except for PE and associated activities).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts; low-cut tops; crop-tops; vest/spaghetti-strap tops.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Clothing with tears, holes, or rips; clothes bearing offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Indoor wearing of baseball caps or hats; high-heeled stiletto shoes or beach flip-flops (indoors).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment (loose pockets, sequins, large buttons).

### Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

* Underwear worn in a revealing manner.
raw_response: {"facts":[{"title":"Inappropriate short clothing","content":"Staff members are not permitted to wear mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts that are shorter than mid-thigh at work.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]},{"title":"Leisure and sportswear","content":"Staff members must not wear leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear unless they are participating in Physical Education or related activities.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]},{"title":"Inappropriate tops","content":"Staff members are prohibited from wearing transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts. Additionally, low-cut tops, crop-tops, and vest or spaghetti-strap tops are not allowed.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]},{"title":"Damaged clothing and offensive graphics","content":"Staff members must avoid wearing clothing with tears, holes, or rips and should not wear clothes that bear offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]},{"title":"Headwear and inappropriate shoes indoors","content":"Staff members are not allowed to wear baseball caps, hats, high-heeled stiletto shoes, or beach flip-flops indoors during work hours.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]},{"title":"Clothing safety","content":"Staff members should avoid wearing clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment, such as clothes with loose pockets, sequins, or large buttons.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]},{"title":"Proper wearing of underwear","content":"Staff members must ensure that their underwear is not worn in a manner that is revealing or inappropriate during work hours.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["dress-code","staff-dress-code"]}]}

facts.title: Inappropriate short clothing
facts.content: Staff members are not permitted to wear mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts that are shorter than mid-thigh at work.

facts.title: Leisure and sportswear
facts.content: Staff members must not wear leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear unless they are participating in Physical Education or related activities.

facts.title: Inappropriate tops
facts.content: Staff members are prohibited from wearing transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts. Additionally, low-cut tops, crop-tops, and vest or spaghetti-strap tops are not allowed.

facts.title: Damaged clothing and offensive graphics
facts.content: Staff members must avoid wearing clothing with tears, holes, or rips and should not wear clothes that bear offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.

facts.title: Headwear and inappropriate shoes indoors
facts.content: Staff members are not allowed to wear baseball caps, hats, high-heeled stiletto shoes, or beach flip-flops indoors during work hours.

facts.title: Clothing safety
facts.content: Staff members should avoid wearing clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment, such as clothes with loose pockets, sequins, or large buttons.

facts.title: Proper wearing of underwear
facts.content: Staff members must ensure that their underwear is not worn in a manner that is revealing or inappropriate during work hours.
